We recommended and Management agreed to require:

1)  The full implementation of PPAs under the LCPC fund to contribute to the attainment of the advocacy for the promotion of the rights and welfare of the children;

2)  The submission of the following reports and onwards ensure their regular submission to facilitate evaluation of the proper recording, reporting and utilization of LCPC funds, and:

2.1	Sangguniang Panlungsod Resolution creating the CCPC
2.2	Annual Work and Financial Plan
2.3	Local Development Plan for Children
2.4	Local Investment Plan for Children
2.5	Local Code for Children
2.6	Local State of Children’s Report
2.7	Accomplishment Report
2.8	Children’s Profile

3) The formulation of responsive ordinances and policies consistent with the child-friendly commitments in accordance with pertinent laws, rules and regulations.

Recommendations
We recommended and Management agreed to require:
The City Engineer to conduct inspection/validate the status of identified completed projects and issue an updated Certification of Completion to be used as the basis of the Accounting Office in the reclassification of the CIP accounts to the proper PPE accounts;
The City Accountant to:
a)   Prepare the SL for every project under the CIP accounts based on the source documents, such as disbursement vouchers which shall be reconciled with their respective control account in the GL at the end of every month;
b)   Submit separately to COA copies of the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) to reclassify the completed projects from the CIP to the appropriate PPE accounts; and
c)   Consequently, recognize the corresponding depreciation of the PPE from the date of completion. Depreciation expenses is computed for one month if the PPE is available for use on or before the 15th of the month, and on the succeeding month if the PPE is available for use after the 15th of the month.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to require the City Accountant to effect the recognition of all the public infrastructure assets back in the books in accordance with IPSAS 45 and the prescribed RCA for LGUs and submit separately a copy of the JEV to COA.

Recommendations
We recommended and Management agreed that:
The City GSO develop an internal control policy or agreement for effective property management and accountability of all PPE charged against the SEF to establish their actual existence and condition purportedly issued to DepEd workforces, and ensure that:
Accountability over issued property items is established at any point in time;
The Property Acknowledgment Receipt (PAR) for Equipment and Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) for Semi-expendable properties be updated regularly or whenever there is a transfer of accountability over the item;
The existence and condition of the PPE items are reasonably determined and physically inspected every year; and
The Accounting Office disclose in the NFS the transfers/adjustment made to support the reconciliation of PPEs from the beginning and the ending of the year.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to implement the one-time cleansing of dormant accounts of under COA Circular No. 2023-008 and observe the proper procedures to write off dormant receivable accounts, unliquidated cash advances, fund transfers, etc. to ensure the fair presentation of the accounts in the FS.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to require the Accounting Office to continue exerting extra effort to trace the unidentified, negative and/or long outstanding beginning balances, verify the supporting documents to establish its validity, reconcile the amounts between the controlling account, the GLs, and the SLs, and effect the necessary adjusting and/or correcting entries.

Recommendations
We reiterated our recommendation that Management requires:
The Accountant and the GSO to strictly adopt the perpetual inventory system of recording the inventory items and coordinate with each other towards its proper implementation to ensure proper accounting and promote better control in the receipt and issuance of supplies and materials; and
The Accounting Office to submit the list of Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) under the dormant FOL inventory account to facilitate their retrieval and evaluation of the transactions for adjustment/issuance, considering that such fuel items were already fully consumed.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed that the City Accountant effect the necessary adjustments and onwards ensure that the accrued interest expense and payable are recorded in accordance with the accrual basis of accounting as required under the IPSAS.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to require the Accounting Office to continue looking into the status of the reconciling items, both in book and bank accounts, prepare the necessary analysis so as to have a basis for the necessary correction and/or adjustment to ensure validity and accuracy of the cash in bank balances.

Recommendation
We recommended and the Management agreed to require the concerned Accounting and Budget Office to conform to the rules and regulations on the preparation of the NFS particularly for the Investment Property account to ensure completeness of information FS.

Recommendations
We recommended and Management agreed to require the GFPS to:
Establish and implement capacity development programs as identified in the annual GPB intended to strengthen the GFPS, TWG, and GAD Secretariat, and to capacitate the LGU officials and employees on fundamental GAD areas. This may include, but are not limited to, training or seminar on GAD Planning and Budgeting, Gender Analysis, Gender Audit and other Gender Analysis Tools such as Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG), Gender Mainstreaming Evaluation Framework (GMEF), Gender Responsive LGU (GeRL), etc., and Gender Sensitivity Training; and
Institute mechanism to implement gender mainstreaming in the City’s operation.

Recommendations
We recommended and Management agreed to require the GFPS to:
Incorporate in the GAD AR the complete appropriations and expenditures of the GAD funds, and reflect all GAD activities undertaken with accurate actual costs of implementation to determine the extent of gender mainstreaming of the City Government;
Ensure that GAD-attributed programs/projects of the City are subjected HGDG for the proper assessment of their gender-responsiveness and determination of the actual expenditure that may be attributed to the GAD budget; and
Prepare and submit the GAD AR in accordance with the guidelines set forth in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC 2013-01, as amended by JMC. 2016-01, and ensure the submission of the necessary documents/reports provided in Section 4.1.C.8.(5) of the same circular.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to establish an Official City Government Website and use modern technology in promoting transparency and accountability in fiscal administration, increasing people’s awareness on the city’s PPAs, appropriations and expenditures, among others, and encourage people’s participation through an embedded feedback mechanism.

Recommendations
We recommended and Management agreed to submit the Physical and Financial Report of Operations of the Pagadian City Infirmary and the Pagadian City International College as required under LBC 111 to facilitate evaluation of the viability of subject economic enterprises and/or public utilities.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to require the joint efforts of the Local Finance Committee (LFC) and the City Economic Enterprise Department Office (CEEDO) to revisit the mechanics on the operation of these economic enterprise to evaluate and formulate strategies that could improve their financial condition, to ensure their vitality so as to return value for the money spent by the City for their operations.

Recommendations
We recommended and Management agreed to:
Faithfully implement the PPAs under the 20 per cent DF, whether Continuing or Current Year appropriation, to achieve the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.
Coordinate with the LDC in the monitoring and evaluation of the implementation of projects to ensure that the development programs and projects are appropriately identified and timely implemented; and
Assess the absorptive capacity of the City to implement the projects, including those under 20 percent DF and adopt measures to address the existing conditions.

Recommendations
We recommended and Management agreed that the LSB:
Maximize the utilization of SEF by integrating programs identified in DepEd-DILG-DBM JCs 01 and 02, s.2020; and
Include allocations from the SEF the implementation of ECCD PPAs in compliance ith DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1 s. 2017.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to maximize the implementation of the LDRRM PPAs to achieve the purpose for which the fund was appropriated and in order for the constituents to enjoy the benefits that could be derived therefrom.

Recommendations
We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed to:
Require the full implementation of the PPAs under the SC Program thru the OSCA Head with the assistance of the CSWDO to contribute to the attainment of the advocacy for the benefits and privileges of the concerned constituents, as a whole;
Require the OSCA to regularly submit the Accomplishment/Utilization Reports to facilitate evaluation of the proper recording, reporting and utilization of SC with reference/JEV Numbers of the disbursements made for the period/year; and
Consider full implementation of the remuneration of the OSCA Head in consonance with RA 9994.

Recommendations
We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed to require the Accounting Office to coordinate with other concerned office/s, such as:
The City Planning and Engineering Offices to:
Verify the status of fund transfers to determine the feasibility of implementing the corresponding projects stated in the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA). Otherwise, return the remaining balance to the source agency; and
Perform regular review and monitoring of accomplishments/ performance of projects funded by the NGAs to ensure that implementation thereof is within the prescribed timeline.
The Engineering Office to facilitate the liquidation of fund transfers for those completed projects; and
The Planning Office to prepare the Report on Government (PPAs) under TF to include all projects funded by NGAs with complete data/information and submit the same to the Office of the City Auditor.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to prioritize the utilization of prior years’ unexpended balance and revert back to the General Fund the amount of LDRRMF identified to be unutilized after 5 years in order to be made available for other social services subject for enactment by the Sangguniang Panlungsod pursuant to the above-cited rules and regulations.

Recommendation
We recommended and Management agreed to faithfully implement the following roles of the LGUs to ensure that the objectives of CBMS are achieved, which is eventually, to support the National Government’s policy of poverty alleviation as embodied in RA 11315:
The quality and consistent data collection, which is to be conducted every three (3) years; and
The monitoring and evaluation of the CBMS to facilitate its integration in the City’s planning, investment programing, budgeting, program identification and implementation, as well as disaster risk reduction and management measures.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the City Accountant to:

1)  Prepare and maintain the Local Road Network Ledger Card for the road and its components namely: a) road lot, b) road pavement, c) drainage and slope protection structures and d) other miscellaneous structures;

2)  Prepare and submit the lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each road component at the end of the year; and

3)  Disclose the total road network system and its important details in the NFS.

We recommended and Management agreed to cause the concerned Disbursing Officers to submit liquidation and/or settlement of their unliquidated cash advances, otherwise, the necessary sanction for its settlement such as the withholding of the salary to be applied to the unliquidated cash advances shall be enforced, and/or the filing of appropriate charges, as warranted, as provided in above-mentioned laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

1)  Require the City Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct and /or restate the balances of affected accounts in the comparative FS in the ensuing year; and

We recommended and Management agreed to:

1) Require the transfer of AREs to the concerned officials who are actually using the motor vehicles or to whom the vehicle was issued and adhere strictly to the rules and regulation on the use, issuance/assignment of service vehicles; and

2) AF Custodian to locate/produce the 1,036 pieces of missing accountable forms and ensure that all unused AFs under his custody be kept in a locked cabinet or secured storage room. In the event that these can no longer be located/presented, a request for relief from accountability for the loss of AFs shall be made in accordance with the requirements under COA Circular No. 84-233.

1) Thru the City Treasurer’s Office to require the concerned AOs to render an accounting of their cash and accounts and submit the same including the turn-over of accountabilities between the outgoing and incoming AOs, to the Office of Auditor in order for the latter to conduct the required cash examination in accordance with pertinent provisions of PD 1445 and the COA Memorandum No. 2013-004 dated July 9, 2013; and

2) Thru the Human Resource Management Office (HRMO) to regularly furnish copies to the COA Office of the list of retiring government employee at least a month prior to the retirement date and list of employees extending their services beyond compulsory retirement age of 65 that are duly approved by the Civil Service Commission (CSC).

We recommended that Management:

1) Facilitate the liquidation of fund transfers with completed projects; 

2) Prepare the Report on Government PPAs under Trust Fund to include all projects funded by NGAs with complete data/information and submit the same to the COA Office; and

3) Perform regular review and monitoring of accomplishments/ performance on projects funded by the NGAs to ensure that implementation thereof is within the prescribed timeline

We recommended and Management agreed to require the LSB to:

1) Prepare and submit the Budget within the reglementary period, using the SEF Budget Preparation Form No.1 which shall be presented by program, activity and project (P/A/P), by allotment class and per school;

Create a new Inventory Committee, not just comprising of employees from General Services Office – for property custodianship, but also from City Accounting – for records and valuation, City Engineers Office – for technical assistance, and other relevant offices with adequate members to be able to conduct a complete physical inventory within the timeframe specified;
b. The new Inventory Committee submit the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) for  the conduct of complete physical inventory which includes the (a) time frame to complete inventory count and the (b) preparation of the report taking into consideration the present condition and location of the properties. (Sections 5.9, 5.10 and 5.11)
c. Continue coordinating with DepEd for the transfer of properties still being maintained in the books of the City but used by and for the operation of the various schools and/or offices of the City Schools Division, conduct proper inventory and turnover thereof, to facilitate derecognition of subject PPE in the City’s books of accounts.

3.14  We recommended that management:
a. 	Continue to find ways and means to implement the asset method of recording the inventory items and require the coordination between the City Accountant and General Services Officer towards the City’s proper implementation of the perpetual inventory method of recording all inventory items to ensure proper accounting and promote better control in the receipt and issuance of supplies and materials.
b.   Determine whether the reported balances, which remained non-moving from beginning to end, are actually on hand and existing.
c.    We also reiterate our recommendation for the Accounting Unit to submit the list of DVs under the dormant FOL Inventory account to facilitate their retrieval and evaluation of the transaction for adjustment/issuance, considering that such fuel items were already fully consumed.

4.12  We recommended that management require the:
Evaluation of the status of the amounts which are more than two years already to properly dispose the accounts, whether they be paid or reverted back to the General Fund Proper, if found to be without valid claims;
Proper listing of the Accounts Payable at the end of the year to include only the valid claims that is, the duly approved Disbursement Vouchers with complete supporting documents; and
Submission of the status of subject infrastructure projects to ensure its proper recording based on the total consolidated cost as a whole.

5.5   We recommended that management require the Accounting Unit to continue looking into the status of the reconciling items, both in book and bank accounts, prepare the necessary analysis so as to have a basis for the necessary correction and/or adjustment to ensure correctness and accuracy of the cash in bank balances.

6.8  We recommended that the City Accountant draw a JEV to reclassify the transactions from the Other Payable account to the appropriate liability account and provide details/breakdown therefor for proper reference.

7.13 We recommended that management require the Accounting Unit to continue exerting extra effort to trace the unidentified, negative and/or long outstanding beginning balances, verify the supporting documents to establish its validity, reconcile the amounts between the controlling account, the General Ledger (GL), and the SL, and effect the necessary adjusting and/or correcting entries, thereof, to have a fair presentation of accounts, and so as not to defeat the purpose of the FS in providing vital information to its users.

8.6    We recommended that the City Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting  journal entries to present the proper accounts and their correct amounts in the books to have a fair presentation in the FS.

10.7  We recommended that management require the:
Creation and maintenance of a SAGF duly sanctioned by the Sanggunian for the receipt and utilization of the BGCM with complete and separate subsidiary ledgers, trial balance and financial statements, which are to be submitted at the end of the year; and
Local Finance Committee thru the City Accountant to prepare the monthly Report on Fund Utilization and Status of PPA Implementation to provide the breakdown of disbursements per Program/Project/Activity, and submit a copy to the Office of the Auditor on a monthly basis.

11.8  We recommended that management require the regular reporting of the actual amounts obligated, disbursed, unutilized balance and the proper disclosure thereof to determine the propriety of the fund utilization of the BGCM grant and the correctness of the amount to be reverted to the National Treasury, if any.

12.7 We recommended that the BAC comply with the guidelines and procedures on Negotiated Procurement through Emergency Cases set forth in the GPBB Circular 01-2020 under the Bayanihan Act to ensure transparency and accountability in the implementation of government procurement during the COVID – 19 pandemic.

13.5  We recommended that Management observe the DOH AO No. 163 series of 2002 in requesting and buying drugs and medicines. The requisitioning officer in the CHO should draw a certification that the products procured by them fall within context of the PNF. Ensure that only those drugs and medicines that conform are listed in the Essential Drugs List of the PNF should be requested and procured, otherwise, the CHO should request with written justification to the Formulary Executive Council for the exceptions.

14.9  We recommended that management require the Local Finance Committee to evaluate the:
City’s financial operations relative to its Personal Services requirements and implement a “compensation scheme that shall take into consideration the financial capability of the City and shall give due regard to the efficient allocation of funds for personnel services which shall be maintained at realistic level in proportion to the overall expenditure of the city,” (Section 1(e), RA 11466), to boost officers and employees’ morale and productivity;
Personnel requirement of the public utilities and economic enterprises of the city to warrant its exclusion in the computation of the PS limitation; and
Relevance and sustainability of the economic enterprises of the City in accordance with the DBM’s 2016 Manual on the Setting Up and Operation of the Local Economic Enterprises, so as not to defeat the purpose of establishing such economic enterprises.
15.5 We recommended that management require the Local Finance Committee to continue the efforts of generating locally-sourced revenues to help augment the City’s IRA-based revenues.

16.11 We recommended that management faithfully adhere to the policies and procedures in coming up with a supplemental budget or for any changes in the approved Annual Budget, especially for special funds like the 20 per cent DF, by proper evaluation of the status of PPAs, which will be the basis in reverting programs and projects or to establish savings therefrom as source of appropriation, so as not to defeat the purpose of achieving the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes as envisioned by City Officials within their term of office.

17.9  We recommended that the City prioritize the establishment of the CDRRMO by duly filling-up the positions of the CDRRM Officer and staff following the qualification standards, position titles and salary grades set by the IRR of RA No. 10121 and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 as these are mandatory positions to provide adequate support to the various City DRRM activities.

18.6  We recommended that the Local School Board formulate a three-year program indicating strategic prioritization policies in the allocation of SEF to ensure a more strategic and efficient utilization of resources for priority programs and projects, which would complement, as well as reinforce, the budgeting priorities at the different levels of decision-making, both in the DepEd and the City Government, pursuant to DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017.

19.5	 We recommended that management require the concerned personnel in-charge to properly deduct and withhold taxes due in the procurement of goods and services in accordance with BIR RR No. 2-98, as amended, and other applicable tax laws in order to avoid penalties that may be imposed for non-compliance, which are personal in nature and shall be borne by the officer responsible thereof.

29.12 We recommended that management conduct evaluation of the operations of the C3, including the proper recording and reporting of its financial transactions, ensure its viability, so as not to defeat the purpose of its creation, which is revenue-generation.
29.13 Consider using “The Manual on Setting Up and Operation of Local Economic Enterprises” under DBM Local Budget Circular No. 111 for its evaluation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Accounting Unit to exert extra effort to trace the unidentified beginning balances, verify the supporting documents to establish its validity, and effect the necessary adjusting and/or correcting entries, thereof, to have a fair presentation of accounts, and so as not to defeat the purpose of the FS in providing vital information to its users; and onwards, observe proper recording of transactions with complete supporting documents, and maintain respective records that in turn will support the existence and correct valuation of accounts in the FS.

We recommended that management require the Accounting Unit to exert extra effort to conduct regular monitoring and analysis of receivable accounts to ensure that these are collected when these become due and demandable, for authorized receivables, and that CA and fund transfers are liquidated within the prescribed period depending upon their nature and purpose, for valid CA.  Otherwise, same are subject to immediate refund by the liable persons.
Consider the requirements for write-off under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, which prescribes the guidelines and procedures in reconciling and cleaning the books of accounts of National Government Agencies (NGAs), Local Government Units (LGU), and Government Owned and/or Controlled Corporations (GOCCs) of dormant receivable accounts, unliquidated CA, and fund transfers.

We recommended that management require the Accounting Unit to exert extra effort to reconcile the amounts between the controlling account, the General Ledger (GL), and the SL, and effect the necessary adjusting and/or correcting entries, thereof, to have a fair presentation of accounts in the FS; and also, onwards, observe correct recording of transactions with complete supporting documents, maintain respective records and practice regular reconciliation of accounts, to ensure integrity of the accounting records and reports, that in turn will support the existence and correct valuation of accounts in the FS.

We recommended that management require:
The C3 management to submit an updated report, and a periodic report thereafter, a Schedule of Due and Demandable CUSA, showing, among others, the amount to be collected, actual collections and the corresponding amount for collection, to serve as basis of the LFC to properly account for the CUSA and other related accounts; and
The revisit of the C3 Manual for possible enhancement of policies, procedures, guidelines, for the proper accounting of the receipts and disbursements of CUSA.

We recommended that management require:
The Accounting and Budget Office to maintain proper recording for every loan obtained using the proper account codes as prescribed presently in the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs, together with the necessary SL and/ or schedules;
The City Accountant to submit a separate and updated quarterly Schedule of Loan Amortization on a regular basis to serve as basis for the Local Finance Committee (LFC) to prepare the City’s Annual Budget for the debt servicing requirements in the ensuing year and for the Accounting Office to make the necessary adjustments in the recorded loan amortizations, considering changes in the interest rates, if any; and
The LFC to ensure that disbursements out of loan proceeds are fully documented and all loan related transactions are recorded and monitored.

We recommended that management require the LFC to intensify the collection of RPT, especially from delinquent taxpayers, as this are already included in the income estimates; ensure that the estimated receipts and appropriations are in amounts approved, reviewed and recorded in the books, where they shall be compared with actual collections and disbursements for the same period; and conduct a semi-annual review and general examination of expenditures and actual accomplishments in the operations of the City, as a whole, to come up with effective ways and means to accomplish PPAs in an efficient, effective and economical manner.

We recommended that management require the concerned SDO to submit liquidation and/or settlement of their unliquidated CA, otherwise, the necessary sanction such as the withholding of the salary to be applied to the unliquidated CA shall be enforced, and/or the filing of appropriate charges, if warranted, as provided in above-mentioned laws, rules and regulations, be made.

We recommended that the concerned SDO properly maintain a cashbook as their cash records for the proper recording and accounting of their cash transactions to facilitate determination of their accountabilities and ensure accurate cash balances, in accordance with Section 28, Chapter I, Volume II of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, which provides that:
“A newly appointed or designated AO shall start with a new cashbook.  Before discharging his duties, the new AO shall be briefed by the Accountant and the Auditor on the proper recording of the transactions and other matters related to his work.”

We recommended that management require the review of the CA of the regular and special DOs, determine the necessity of granting the CA, activities and/or transactions subject of a cash advance and properly compute the proper fidelity bond of the concerned accountable officers based on cash accountabilities handled and in accordance with the BTR Circular No. 02-2009 dated August 6, 2009, not only to ensure proper indemnification in case of defalcations, shortages, and/or unrelieved losses in the accounts of bonded public officers, but also to avoid unnecessary expenses in the form of bond premiums.

We recommended that management adhere strictly to the rules and regulation on the proper custody, utilization and issuance or assignment of service vehicles and as such, submit an updated List of Motor Vehicles, with the corresponding assigned personnel or officials, among other data, to properly determine the person/s accountable, responsible and liable for the upkeep of subject vehicles.

We recommended that management provide budgetary requirements for the operation of the CDRRMO under the GF in accordance with Section 4.3 of NDRRM-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-01, to ensure that LDRRM funds will be used primarily for activities within the four (4) thematic areas of disaster risk reduction and management such as disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response and rehabilitation and recovery.

We recommended that the City comply with the existing regulations for the creation and filling up of plantilla positions under the CDRRMO required to perform the functions of administration and training, research and planning and operations and warning in accordance with RA 10121 to ensure that DRRM programs, projects and activities will be implemented in an effective, efficient and economical manner.

c.   Management establish a specific policy and operational guidelines to implement the modified accrual basis of accounting, not only for income but also for expenditures, after evaluating the effects thereof to the respective accounts, to improve the quality, coverage, timeliness, and relevance of the City’s financial data and ensure compliance with the PPSAS.

d.  Management revisit contracts with stall owners to determine whether C3 stalls were sold or merely leased out as a means of checking on the correctness of account balances, and preparing adjustments thereon.

The City must ensure that its land are free from legal issues before facilities are constructed thereon; that the properties with legal impediments be immediately acted on by the City Legal Department and for the latter to maintain a record/custody of all the legal documents pertaining to the properties acquired, whether by cash or donation.
The City appropriate funds for the titling of all its land centralized under the CGSO instead of under the City Legal Office or under the respective government offices involved in a particular project as what is currently practiced.

We recommended that management faithfully comply with existing laws, rules and regulations on the use of government motor transportation to support the legality, validity and propriety of the transactions, and the reasonableness of FOL consumption.
Management should consider devising a system wherein the fuel is controlled and monitored centrally by the CGSO and not by the individual departments as practiced, such that each utilization is authorized and controlled and payments are for actual billings.

We recommended that management in coordination with the City Engineer’s Office (CEO) conduct inspection of infrastructure projects before and after completion of the projects until the end of the DLP including the submission of reports relative thereto to serve as basis in the issuance of Certificate of Final Acceptance of infrastructure projects and the release of the Retention Money or warranty, if any.

We recommended that:
The City Planning and Development Office (CPDO) in coordination with the City Budget Office (CBO) and the CEO ensure the complete submission at the beginning of the year of the list of ongoing PPAs that are to be implemented during the year;
The CEO to formulate policies, systems or processes on how to comply with the above-cited Circular which also includes monitoring and evaluation on the completed projects which have not yet been accepted, and already accepted infrastructures, for possible design and structural defects; and
The concerned/assigned office and/or personnel to ensure that project signboards and/or public notices are posted and updated as current as possible.

We recommended that management direct the City Accountant and other officers responsible in determining the income payments of personnel engaged under JO or COS arrangement to withhold taxes, SSS contributions and other deductions in accordance with tax laws and other applicable laws, rules and regulations to help the government in its operations and so as not to defeat the purpose of the SSS program for personnel not covered by the GSIS law.
Please be reminded of the provisions that failure of the persons responsible to comply with the provisions relative to withholding, deduction and remittance of taxes on compensation and its year-end adjustment, is a ground for appropriate legal actions and is subject to penal provisions as provided in Section 272 of RA 8424.

We recommended that management thoroughly plan its personnel complement and give the appropriate compensation to its JO personnel, subject to the usual accounting, auditing and other existing laws, rules and regulations on the matter, to help uplift their living conditions and improve productivity, that will redound to the benefit of the City Government and the general public who they serve.

We recommended that the City Government issue policy guidelines ion the hiring of personnel under JO/ COS in accordance with CSC, Revised Omnibus Rules, COA and other existing labor laws, rules and regulations, to give the workers, both regular and casual/ contractual/ JO, the benefits due them, define their accountabilities and contribute to the overall productivity of the City Government.
Local Budget Circular (LBC) No. 112 requires the submission of Local Budget Preparation (LBP) Form No. 3 - Personnel Schedule by the Department Head and reviewed by the Local Finance Committee (LFC) for consolidation and review. From these, the City Planning Coordinator, CHRMO and City Budget Office must set plans and come up with an Organizational Structure such that all vacant positions are filled-up, instead of resorting to JO/ COS scheme, and include in the personnel schedule those appointed as co-terminus, JOs and COS.
Further, if merited by applicable CSC regulations, JO/ COS personnel should be evaluated for possible appointment to permanent regular positions.

We recommended that Management prepare a service contract agreement according to the prescribed format provided per DOLE laws, rules and regulations. The City as indirect employers of the security, janitors and slaughterhouse personnel of PRAC SMSA should endeavor to ensure compliance with mandated employment benefits and working conditions to secure the rights and social protection benefits of the contracted personnel.

We recommended that:
The City thru the SP formulate additional criteria/policies or guidelines in the granting of Financial Assistance to Individuals/Families in Crisis Situation to serve as basis in the amount to be extended per nature of assistance needed;
The City Mayor grant authority in the maintenance of a revolving fund in the form of a special cash advance by a regular Disbursing Officer with a minimum amount which shall be made available for the purpose of expediting delivery of basic services under the Assistance to Individuals in Crisis Situation (AICs) program; and
Management coordinates with partner agencies such as funeral parlors, drugstores/pharmacy, hospitals and other government agencies where clients may be referred for assistance through a MOA.

We recommended that the City DRRMO include in the LDRRMFIP, in a separate caption, the list of projects and activities to be funded from the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years, coordinate with the City Accountant and City Budget Office on the actual unexpended balances of the previous years’ LDRRMF - that is, the 30% QRF, 70% MOOE and Continuing Appropriations-Completed Projects for the correct presentation of unexpended balances.
We also recommended that management implement the projects in the LDRRMFIP which are intended to contribute in strengthening the capacity of the City Government to respond to disasters.

We recommended that the City prioritize the establishment of the CDRRMO by duly filling-up the positions of the CDRRM Officer and staff following the qualification standards, position titles and salary grades set by Rule 6, Sections 2 and 3 of IRR of RA 10121 and Items 5.2, 5.3 and 5.4 of NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1 as these are mandatory positions, and to ensure that DRRM plans and policies are properly developed and implemented.
It may also help that “in accordance with Section 83 of RA 10352, the enforcement of Sections 325(a) and 331(b) of RA 7160 shall be waived to enable the LGUs to fund the initial year requirement for the creation of the minimum four (4) mandatory positions at the LDRRMO.”

It is recommended that:

a.  The City Accountant reclassify the account Other Business Income relating to the collections of sales and rentals of the C3 to account Trust Liabilities in accordance with the C3 guidelines as also defined in the Revised Philippine Chart of Accounts (PRCA) for LGUs.

b.  The C3 Manager submit a status report, copy furnished COA as of December 31, 2016 and yearly thereafter, both for stalls for rent and for sale.

Such report will be one of the basis of the Accounting Office to take up the Receivable account for the proceeds of the sales, rent and other collection of the C3, taking into consideration that such proceeds will still have to be taken up in the books as Trust Liability and not yet as Income as provided in the C3 regulations.

The report can also be used as basis for the Treasury Office and/or the Finance Committee in the projection and/or estimates of income in the preparation of the City’s Annual Budget. 

Prior years’ (PY) collectibles should also be properly reported and accounted for in the books of accounts.  This is one of COA’s PY observation per CY 2013 AAR, where we requested for the submission of a status report on the following PY audit recommendations for management to:

1.) Demand from the stallholders and enforce payment on their accounts to increase the collection efficiency of the C3;

2.) Device measures/ programs to improve the collection rate of the C3; and

3.)  Strictly impose the provision of Article VIII, Section 1 (m) of City Ordinance No. 2K9-288.

c. The City Accountant to:

1.) Properly record the expenses of the C3 operations on its actual incurrence, with proper account codes and classification taking into consideration that such disbursements still have to be taken up in the books as debit to the Trust Liability account;

2.) Submit a separate and updated Schedule of Loan Amortization for the C3 loans to serve as basis for the Finance Committee to prepare the C3 Annual Budget, and make the necessary adjustments of the recorded loan amortizations, to show the correct amounts actually paid for the principal and the loan interest.

3.) Maintain a separate Subsidiary Ledger (SL) for the C3 Cash In Bank (CIB) account and prepare the corresponding breakdown in the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) for this.

4.) Submit the corrected FS for the C3 operation to ensure that the financial transactions are properly accounted for, and that the result of its operation and the status of its financial position (assets, liabilities and equity) are fairly presented in the reports for it to be useful in the economic decisions of the City Government, in particular, and the different stakeholders, as a whole.

d. The City Budget Officer properly present and/or separately report the annual budget and the corresponding plantilla of personnel for the C3, even though consolidated in the GF to facilitate verification, monitoring and reconciliation of the C3 accounts, and eventually qualify as a waived item in the computation of the PS limitation.

e. The corresponding Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) items should be recorded in the C3 books of accounts to ensure proper and fair presentation of accounts in the FS.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

a.   Stop the granting of honoraria to the concerned C3 personnel;

b.  Refrain from paying claims of this nature in the future to avoid disallowance in audit; and

c. Adhere to existing rules and regulations in the grant of honoraria to ensure the legality of transactions.  Agency heads shall be held responsible and personally liable for honoraria payments contrary to the pertinent provisions of these rules and regulations, without prejudice, to the refunds by the personnel concerned. (Section 6.0, DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-2 dated October 1, 2007)

Auditor’s Rejoinder:

The operation of the C3 as an EE is a government function (Section 313 (c), RA 7160) and the funds used for its operation are government funds.  As such, the officials and employees assigned in its operations, who are justly compensated, are not entitled to an honorarium as additional compensation.
observations.managementAction:
Management justified the grant of honoraria on the following grounds, although the granting thereof was already stopped:

1)  The work done by the C3 Board of Directors is not government related as it is not concerned with the basic duties inherent in the regular functions of City Officials and as an agent of the National Government; and

2)  The grant of honoraria is supported by an Ordinance creating the C3 Mall, hence, authorized by law;

The LGU has a two-fold personality: (1) as an agent of the National Government, when it implements national laws, programs or when it is performing basic governmental functions; and as (2) a private juridical person when it assumes its corporate personality, like when it operates an EE.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

b.  Impose control measures to ensure that procurement is effected in an efficient, economical and effective manner that is most advantageous to the government; and

c. Conduct pre-repair evaluation/ inspection and post repair evaluation/ inspection of government vehicles subjected to repairs.

We further recommended that an SL must be maintained by the Accounting Office for the expenses incurred for the repair and maintenance of each vehicle owned by the City.

Auditor’s Rejoinder:

The pre and post – repair inspection functions as a check on the installation of parts of subject properties, regardless on whether the repairs were done by the agency’s own personnel, or by outside supplier.

Management, particularly the BAC, should evaluate historical data so as to plan the procurement of spare parts onwards in accordance with RA 9184 so as not to defeat the purpose of the law to have a judicious use of government resources.
observations.managementAction:
Management reasoned that there was no intention on its part to split requisitions to circumvent public bidding. The BAC could not wait and consolidate the requisitions because the repair of motor vehicles and heavy equipments; and the replacement of their spare parts are considered urgent in nature so as not to hamper the operation of the City Government and the implementation of the various PPAs undertaken.

The spare parts were procured thru Small Value Procurement, although considered per Purchase Request. These were within the threshold of this mode of procurement.

Pre-repair inspection was always followed.  However, the Post-repair inspection was not done since the repairs were done at the City’s Motorpool Division.  The information appearing in the post-repair inspection/ evaluation report is reflected in the work accomplishment report of the concerned office.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
It is recommended that:
a) The City Accountant reclassify the account Other Business Income relating to the
collections of sales and rentals of the C3 to account Trust Liabilities in
accordance with the C3 above-cited guidelines as also defined in the Revised
Philippine Chart of Accounts for LGUs as follows:
Account Title Trust Liabilities
Account Number 2-04-01-010
Normal Balance Credit
Description This account is used to record the receipt of amount held
in trust for specific purpose. Debit this account for
payment or settlement of the liability or compliance of
condition.
b) The C3 Manager submit a status report, copy furnished COA, as of December 31,
2016 and yearly thereafter, both for stalls for rent and for sale, containing the
following data, among others:
1.) Name of Stall Owner
2.) Stall Number (or any stall reference used by management)
3.) Contract Period
4.) Total Amount Due for the Year
5.) Total Amount Collected
5.1 Monthly Rental
5.2 Fees
5.3 Sale
5.4 Others (if any)
6.) Balance for Collection (Receivable)
7.) Remarks (to include, among others, the sanction taken by management on
delinquent payees, if there are any)

Such report will be one of the basis of the Accounting Office to take up the
receivable account for the proceeds of the sales, rent and other collection of the
C3, taking into consideration also that such proceeds will still have to be taken up
in the books as trust liability and not yet income as provided in the previously-
mentioned C3 regulations.

The report can also be used as basis for the Treasury Office and/or the Finance
Committee in the projection and/or estimates of income in the preparation of the
City‟s Annual Budget.

Prior years‟ collectibles should also be properly reported and accounted for in the
books of accounts of the City. This is one of COA‟s prior year observation (CY
2013 AAR, Pages 66-67) and we request submission of status report on the
following prior years‟ audit recommendation that:
Management should:
1.) Make a demand from the stallholders and enforce the payment thereof to
increase the collection efficiency of the City Commercial Center;
2.) Device measures/programs to improve the collection rate of the City
Commercial Center; and
3.) Strictly impose the provision of Article VIII, Section 1 (m) of City Ordinance
No. 2K9-288.
c) The City Accountant properly record the expenses of the C3 operations upon its
actual incurrence, with proper account codes and classification and again taking
into consideration also that such disbursements will still have to be taken up in the
books as debit to the trust liability account for all payments or settlement of the
liability or compliance of the condition as provided in the previously-mentioned
C3 regulations.
d) The City Budget Officer properly present and/or separately report the annual
budget and the corresponding plantilla of personnel for the C3, even though
consolidated in the GF to facilitate verification, monitoring and reconciliation of
the C3 accounts, and eventually qualify as a waived item in the computation of
the PS limitation.
e) The City Accountant submit a separate and updated Schedule of Loan
Amortization for the C3 loans to serve as basis for the Finance Committee in the
preparation of the C3 Annual Budget and for the Accounting Office to make the
necessary adjustments of the recorded loan amortizations, to show the correct
amounts actually paid for the principal and the loan interest.f) The Accounting Office maintain a separate Subsidiary Ledger for the C3 Cash In
Bank account and prepare the corresponding breakdown in the monthly Bank
Reconciliation Statements of subject bank account.
g) The corresponding PPE items should be recorded in the C3 books of accounts to
ensure proper and fair presentation of accounts in the financial statements.
h) The Accounting Office submit the corrected Financial Statements for the C3
operation to ensure that the financial transactions are properly recorded and
accounted for and that the result of its operation and the status of its financial
position (assets, liabilities and equity) are clearly and fairly presented in the
reports for it to be useful in the economic decisions of the City Government, in
particular, and the different stakeholders, as a whole.

Recommendation:
It is recommended that onwards, management strictly enforce the provisions of
COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 in the granting, utilization and liquidation
of cash advances. The concerned officials and employees with cash advances shall effect
immediate settlement of the same, otherwise, the necessary sanction for its settlement
such as the withholding of the salary to be applied to the unliquidated cash advances shall
be enforced, without prejudice to the filing of appropriate charges as provided in COA
Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012 relative to the Demand for Immediate
Liquidation and Settlement of All Cash Advances Outstanding as of December 31, 2011,
which provides:
Failure of an accountable officer to liquidate his outstanding cash
advance on or before January 31, 2013 shall constitute cause for the filing
of malversation charge under Article 217, failure to liquidate cash
advance under Article 218, both of the Revised Penal Code or criminal
prosecution under Section 128 of PD 1445.

Recommendation:
It is recommended that:
a) The Local Finance Committee and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to
specifically identify the nature of services that is eligible as general services with
strict adherence to RA 9184.
b) The Human Resource Management Officer facilitate the execution of a valid
contract between the City and the personnel before the latter will render service
(for individual engagement). Moreover, upon submission of the Department‟s
Accomplishment Report, attachment of the schedule of personnel complement
employed by the City is prescribed.
c) The City Accountant observe stringent fiscal administration in the proper
classification of accounts.
d) The City Government refrain from hiring Job Orders/Contracts of Services unless
these are necessary in the implementation of the local projects. Fill up the
vacancies instead of hiring JOs or maximize the services of available personnel so
as not to incur additional expenses.

Recommendation:
It is recommended that the Local Finance Committee ensures that appropriate
measures are installed to control not only the appropriations, allotments, and cash
availability but proper implementation of PPAs as well. The Treasurer should also
implement control measures in the proper disposition of the LGU‟s cash accounts
particularly cash pertaining to trust and/or special funds received by the City Government
to ensure that:
a) Cash is deposited intact in the proper books of accounts where it belongs, i.e., trust
receipts should be directly received, deposited and recorded in the Trust Fund
books of accounts;
b) Amounts due for remittances, like the BIR, GSIS, etc., should be remitted within
the reglementary period to avoid penalties and/or other sanctions that may be
imposed by the BIR and other regulatory bodies, which are considered as personal
in nature, thus, are personal liability of the concerned officer;
c) Cash disbursements are made only for valid transactions pertaining to the
particular fund to avoid suspension/s and/or disallowance/s in audit;
d) Plans/programs/activities are implemented as planned, prioritized and budgeted in
the intended year; and
e) Adjust and/or correct accordingly the obligations in relation to the actual income
realized as intended by Section 147 (c) of GAAM, Volume I.

Recommendation:

a) Revisit the identified priority development projects intended for CY 2009 to 2016
and verify as to the reason of non-implementation;
b) Initiate the needed coordination, monitoring and evaluation on the implementation
to assure that for CY 2017 all unimplemented projects and programs be given
immediate action and implementation.; and
c) Strictly adhere to the guidelines in the execution of the respective responsibilities
by the LDC, the Executive Committee and of the LCE.
d) Restore the total amount of P536,209.80 realigned from the 20% EDF to the
general fund, the P500,000.00 of which was intended for the purchase of lot for
cemetery and the amount of P36,209.80 was omitted from the SWMP.

Recommendation:
It is recommended that:
a) The City Budget Office prepare and submit the List of Budget Utilizations Not
Yet Due and Demandable (Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses and
Capital Outlay) as of December 31 of each year (Section 3.5, COA Accounting
Circular Letter No. 2007-002 dated 1/19/2007), which represents “obligations
which are not yet recorded or as liabilities but are valid commitments based on
approved contracts/purchase orders which are not yet implemented and/or goods
are not yet delivered;”
b) The City Budget Officer include in the annual budget accurate amount for the debt
services that the City is bound to pay for the budget year with reference to loan
amortization schedules provided by the financial institutions;
c) The City Treasurer‟s Office prepare and submit the List of Accounts Payable,
which represents the Disbursement Voucher and/or Payroll which are duly
processed, approved and ready for payment, i.e. they are obligations due and
demandable;
d) Management instill internal control on the budget, payment of and recording of
their financial transaction and as such:
1) The Local Finance Committee and the Sangguniang Panlungsod, in coming up
with a supplemental budget, should be cautious enough for the necessary
constraints to be considered by adhering to the regulations set forth under RA
7160. When resorting to the reversion of payable accounts, adherence to the
policies and procedures and proper authorization be followed;
2) The City Accounting Office, particularly, the Pre-Audit Division, shall
faithfully comply with one of the fundamental principles governing the
financial transactions and operations of any government agency under Section
4(6) of PD 1445, which provides that, ―Claims against government funds
shall be supported with complete documentation,‖ and ensure that transaction
documents (DVs, Payrolls, PRs, etc), even adjusting entries, should be
completely documented; and
3) The City Accountant to assure compliance and safekeeping of the unpaid
disbursement vouchers and other necessary documents to ascertain the
validity, propriety and legality of the various liabilities account supporting the
accounts in the financial statements; and to facilitate verification thereof.

Recommendation:
It is recommended that the Local School Board:
a) Appropriate funds for project/programs/activities as mandated and authorized by
law and as prioritized under Section 100 of RA 7160;
b) Programs be within what was allowed. Exclude activities/transactions which fall
under the exclusions in LOI 1462 in the application and implementation of
programs;
c) Align activities within the priorities as mandated to assure compliance as to its
appropriation, authority and payment; and
d) Submit the Report of SEF Utilization duly certified by the City Accountant and
the Local Chief Executive to facilitate monitoring thereof.

Recommendation:
It is recommended that the Local School Board:
(a) Determine the annual supplementary budgetary needs for the operation and
maintenance of public schools within the city and the supplementary local cost of
meeting such needs in accordance with the criteria set by the Department of
Education; and
(b) Submit documents to support the proposal showing the criteria of DepEd as the
basis of the supplementary budget together with the budget which was
supplemented.

Recommendation:
It is recommended that management:
a) Faithfully comply with the relevant provisions of the laws, rules and regulations
governing the administration of LDRRMF to be assured of government resources
being administered and used properly, regularly and legally according to the
purposes for which the fund was created;
b) Ensure that all processes and documentations prescribed under other relevant laws
and rules are duly complied with for disbursement to be valid and proper,
necessary and effective in consonance with the purpose sought to be attained;
c) Submit the LDRRMF Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) for CY 2016 and years
thereafter in accordance with Section 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and
the correct Utilization Report (Section 5.1.5) to ensure that LDRRM Funds are
properly recorded and accounted for.

Recommendation:
It is recommended that management:
a) Properly devise programs on how the seminars and training will be conducted in
the future, giving consideration on the austerity measures and saving of
government monies for priority programs as provided under existing LDRRMF
laws, rules and regulations.
b) Follow strictly the reasonable cost per meal or the authorized cost per meal of
P80.00 as provided under EO 298.
c) Submit justification/explanations as to the time frame and the corresponding
disbursements and the legal basis as to the grant of meal allowances.

Recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation to fully implement RA 9003 and its
appropriation in the City‟s Budget. Since closure of the open dumpsite and the
establishment of a sanitary landfill may exceed the present financial and human resource
capacity of the City they may consider income-generating schemes, i.e. fines and
penalties for violations of the act to defray expenses in the implementation of the plan.
Strictly impose waste separation at source and MRFs in partnership with its key
stakeholders: citizens, academe, NGOs and barangays at most in accordance with the
SWMP. For the utilization of the 20% EDF, DILG-DBM JMC 2011-1 and RA 7160 shall
be strictly observed and completeness of supporting documents must be assured.

Recommendation:
It is recommended that management:
a) Submit the required documents as enumerated above and/or submit
explanation/justification to ascertain the legality, validity and propriety of subject
transaction and that onwards, claims should be duly processed and supported with
complete documents before these should be paid to avoid suspensions and/or
disallowance in audit;
b) The Accounting and Budget Offices use the proper account codes as prescribed
presently in the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs under COA Circular No.
2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 so as not to make the City‟s financial reports
misleading; and
c) The Liga ng mga Barangay properly observe the pertinent government accounting
rules and regulations in their operations (Section 505, RA 7160) considering that
subject leagues are already considered government organizations.

submitted to facilitate review/verification of transactions and operations of the Liga ng
mga Barangay-Pagadian City Chapter, to wit:

b. List of Officials

Recommendation:
It is recommended that the CEEDO:
a) prepare all documents as mandated by their duties and functions to come up with
what was the intention and objective of the department; and
b) coordinate with the accounting and property offices for the proper recording of all
properties belonging to each economic enterprise to come up with fair balances
and actual income realized and expenses incurred. Being an economic enterprise,
whether officially sanctioned or not, requires the separate and proper recording of
its financial transactions as a special account in the General Fund.

The city government in order to expand its manning requirement must establish
the identified economic enterprises on the basis of their sustained financial
viability in becoming a revenue generating, self-sustaining and genuine profit
centers.

Recommendation:
It is recommended that management:
a) Stop the granting of honoraria granted to the concerned personnel;
b) Refrain from paying claims of this nature in the future to avoid disallowance in
audit; and
c) Adhere to existing rules and regulations on the grant of honoraria to ensure the
legality of transactions. It is reminded that agency heads shall be held responsible
and personally liable for honoraria payments not in accordance with the
provisions of above-cited circular, without prejudice, however, to the refund by
the personnel concerned. (Section 6.0, DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-2 dated
October 1, 2007)

Recommendation:
It is recommended that management:
a) faithfully adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 and to the above mentioned
circulars;
b) impose control measures to ensure that procurement is effected in an efficient,
economical and effective manner that is most advantageous to the government
and to prevent possible disallowances for such violation; and
c) conduct pre-repair evaluation/inspection and post repair evaluation/inspection of
all its government vehicles subjected to repairs.

We further recommended that a subsidiary ledger must be maintained by the
Accounting Office for the expenses incurred regarding repair and maintenance for every
vehicle owned by the City of Pagadian.

Recommendation:
It is recommended that:
a) Management strictly adhere to the rules and regulations governing the granting of
RATA to the qualified personnel considering the following factors, to avoid
disallowances in audit:
i. Persons authorized/qualified to claim
ii. Amount to be paid which is rightfully due to them based on actual work
performance
iii. Documentary requirements satisfactory to the conditions set forth to determine
the eligibility and ensure equity and uniformity especially to those OICs and
newly designated Department Heads‟ 1st claim for RATA.
b) The City Human Resource Management officer to strictly monitor the designation,
i.e. terms and conditions of appointment to avoid indistinct and redundancy in
accountability, responsibility and privileges of the personnel.
c) The General Services Officer to strictly assess the actual usage and entitlement of
government transportation before issuance of certificate of non-usage and/or
assignment of motor vehicles keeping in mind Section 8.2.1 of LBC No. 103.
d) The City Accountant to faithfully comply with the requirements of the
completeness of supporting documents and proper charges made on the RATA
expense account.

Recommendation:
It is recommended that management should draw a policy/guidelines, with proper
sanctions, on the payment of communication expenses to the City Government‟s
Officials, Department Heads and other qualified personnel but considering the following
factors and in accordance with the above-mentioned laws, rules and regulations, among
other pertinent regulations:
1) persons authorized to claim;
2) amount to be paid should be the amount actually incurred but not to exceed the
reasonable ceiling to be set by management;
3) that expenses incurred are official in nature and is necessary in the performance
of duties and functions and the mandate of the office and the City Government
as a whole; and
4) that disbursements should be advantageous to the government with the end
view of the efficiency, economy and effectiveness with which government
resources are expended.

b.  Review and analyze the account balances of each promissory note (PN), maintain a separate subsidiary ledger (SL) for each PN to facilitate said review and apprise the Sangguniang Panlungsod (SP) about loan accounts that fall due so that it can provide appropriation sufficient to cover the payment of amortizations of the principal and the interests due.

The City should develop a procedural flow as follows:

Upon receipt of an ObR, the budget officer/authorized representative should: 
1. Pre-evaluate the completeness of supporting documents (DV, Contracts, PO, etc.)
2.  Prepare, process and sign the Obligation Request (includes facilitating signing of the OBR by the requisitioning unit)
3.  Record obligation incurred to the (electronic) control file
4.  Record obligation incurred to the respective RAO (separate registries should be maintained).
5. Forward to Accounting Office, the duly signed ObR by the Budget Officer.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management revisit and update the Local Revenue Code and/or amend existing ordinances in accordance with the Local Government Code.  The Local Finance Committee shall consider what provisions of the Revenue Code to be retained, deleted and added to have a sound projections of the LGUs financial requirements.  

Further, updating and rationalizing the tax rates of the Revenue Code once every five years as required under Section 191 of the Local Government Code helps to ensure the sustainability and resilience of various local taxes.  On the other hand, updating the rates for the regulatory/service fees and charges makes these fees and charges commensurate to the cost of regulation and the services provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the City Accountant to book up as Expenses all obligations incurred during the year as required by Section 119 of PD 1445. observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the City Accountant to:

1. Submit the reports and documents relative to the collections of and payments from government funds, and TBs to enable the conduct of timely verification of the accounts. Otherwise, the sanctions as provided in Section 122 of P.D. 1445 shall be imposed; and

2. Submit justification for failure to submit the required reports and documents despite repeated demands. 

Also, the City Budget Officer shall prepare and submit the monthly SAAOs.

We recommend that management require the Accounting Office to ensure that the control measure of numbering the disbursement transactions before processing is adopted, which is a tool for reconciliation on the proper control of appropriation and its utilization duly recorded in the books.  

The personnel in-charge in receiving and controlling/ numbering of all  disbursement transactions must coordinate with the one in-charge of receiving the paid disbursement transactions, to determine the unsubmitted, obligated and processed transactions which remained unpaid as of a given period, to facilitate the reconciliation process.

We recommend that management limit the granting of CAs only to those who are duly designated Dos or SDOs, to those who have no unliquidated CAs, and only for purposes allowable per COA Circular No. 97-002. We recommend the following measures:
1.Instruct the concerned  personnel at the Motor pool thru the City Economic Enterprise Officer to validate and document the receivables of P21,738,460.63, after which, the same shall be booked-up by the City Accountant into the books of accounts.  Intensify the collection of validated and documented receivables.  The failure of the private lessor to pay the rental is a breach of contract. 2. Instruct the City Economic Enterprise Officer to draft an Operating Manual of the Motor Pool for approval of the Chief Executive as guide in its operation.  The same Operating Manual must be approved by the Sangguniang Panlungsod. 
3. The City Economic Enterprise Officer should regularly apprise in writing the Chief Executive, the Sangguniang Panlungsod and other authorities concerned relative to the financial condition and result of operations of the Motor Pool so that appropriate actions in the process will be made.
